name: YouTube Publisher
description: Upload videos to YouTube as private drafts (Shorts and long-form) via the YouTube Data API v3

required_variables.description: Google OAuth2 client ID (from Google Cloud Console)
YouTube OAuth2 refresh token (obtained via initial consent flow)

skill.name: youtube_publish
skill.description: Upload videos to YouTube as private drafts (Shorts and long-form)
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to YouTube upload tools. All uploads are ALWAYS private (draft mode) — you cannot publish publicly.

Use refresh_youtube_token to get a fresh access token before uploading.
Use upload_to_youtube to upload a video file (any length, any aspect ratio) and set its metadata.

VIDEO TYPES — both supported:

- **YouTube Shorts**: vertical (9:16), ≤60 seconds.
  - Pass is_short=true OR let the tool auto-detect (ffprobe checks orientation + duration).
  - The tool will auto-add #Shorts to the description if missing.
  - Use category 22 (People & Blogs) for general content.
  - Keep titles under 100 characters, add 5–10 tags.

- **Long-form videos** (anything else — 15–45 min ASMR, vlogs, tutorials, music, etc.):
  - Pass is_short=false, OR simply let the tool auto-detect.
  - The tool will NOT add #Shorts (so the video is NOT classified as a Short).
  - Title up to 100 chars, description can be long, 10–30 tags.
  - Choose the appropriate categoryId (e.g. 24 = Entertainment, 22 = People & Blogs, 28 = Science & Tech, 10 = Music).

For video_url, pass either a local file path (e.g. /tmp/at-compose-XXX/video.mp4) or an HTTPS URL — the tool handles both.

AUTO-DETECTION: if you don't pass is_short, the tool runs ffprobe on the file and treats the video as a Short ONLY when it is vertical (height ≥ width) AND ≤60 seconds; otherwise it uploads as long-form. If ffprobe is unavailable, the tool defaults to long-form (safer — avoids false-positive Shorts classification).

RESILIENCE: the tool uses YouTube's resumable upload with extended timeouts (up to 4 hours) and curl retries, so large long-form files (hundreds of MB, 30+ minute ASMR, etc.) won't fail with 'video too long' or premature-EOF errors. The returned URL is /shorts/<id> for Shorts and /watch?v=<id> for long-form videos.

All uploads are PRIVATE drafts — review and publish manually from YouTube Studio.

tools.name: refresh_youtube_token
tools.description: Refresh the YouTube OAuth2 access token using the stored refresh token

tools.name: upload_to_youtube
tools.description: Upload a video to YouTube as a private draft. Supports BOTH Shorts and long-form videos. Call refresh_youtube_token first to get an access_token. Supports both local file paths and URLs for video_url. Auto-detects Short vs long-form via ffprobe unless is_short is set. Videos are ALWAYS uploaded as private.

video_url.description: Path to the video file (local path like /tmp/at-compose-XXX/video.mp4) or HTTPS URL to download

description.description: Video description. For Shorts, #Shorts is auto-added; for long-form videos, do NOT include #Shorts.

category_id.description: YouTube category ID. Common values: 22 (People & Blogs, default), 24 (Entertainment), 28 (Science & Tech), 10 (Music), 27 (Education).

is_short.description: Optional. true = upload as YouTube Short (adds #Shorts, returns /shorts/ URL). false = long-form video (no #Shorts, returns /watch?v= URL). If omitted, the tool auto-detects via ffprobe (vertical AND ≤60s = Short).

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

ACCESS_TOKEN="$ARG_ACCESS_TOKEN"
VIDEO_URL="$ARG_VIDEO_URL"
VISIBILITY="private"
CATEGORY_ID="${ARG_CATEGORY_ID:-22}"
IS_SHORT_ARG="$ARG_IS_SHORT"

if [ -z "$ACCESS_TOKEN" ] || [ -z "$VIDEO_URL" ] || [ -z "$ARG_TITLE" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Missing required parameters: access_token, video_url, and title are required"}'
  exit 0
fi

# Create temp directory
WORKDIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}/at-youtube-$(python3 -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:10])')"
mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
VIDEO_FILE="$WORKDIR/video.mp4"

# Get video file — handle local paths and URLs
if [ -f "$VIDEO_URL" ]; then
  cp "$VIDEO_URL" "$VIDEO_FILE"
elif echo "$VIDEO_URL" | grep -q '^file://'; then
  LOCAL_PATH=$(echo "$VIDEO_URL" | sed 's|^file://||')
  cp "$LOCAL_PATH" "$VIDEO_FILE"
elif echo "$VIDEO_URL" | grep -q '^/'; then
  cp "$VIDEO_URL" "$VIDEO_FILE"
else
  curl -sL --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 1800 -o "$VIDEO_FILE" "$VIDEO_URL"
fi

if [ ! -f "$VIDEO_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$VIDEO_FILE" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Failed to get video file"}'
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

VIDEO_SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$VIDEO_FILE" 2>/dev/null || stat -f%z "$VIDEO_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)

# Determine Short vs long-form.
# Auto-detect (vertical AND <=60s) only when is_short not explicitly set.
# Defaults to long-form (safer — avoids accidental Shorts classification on long videos).
IS_SHORT="false"
if [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "true" ] || [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "1" ] || [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "yes" ]; then
  IS_SHORT="true"
elif [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "false" ] || [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "0" ] || [ "$IS_SHORT_ARG" = "no" ]; then
  IS_SHORT="false"
elif command -v ffprobe >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  W=$(ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=width  -of csv=p=0 "$VIDEO_FILE" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | tr -d '\r')
  H=$(ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=height -of csv=p=0 "$VIDEO_FILE" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | tr -d '\r')
  D=$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 "$VIDEO_FILE" 2>/dev/null | head -n1 | tr -d '\r')
  IS_SHORT=$(W="$W" H="$H" D="$D" python3 -c "
import os
try:
    w=int(float(os.environ.get('W') or 0))
    h=int(float(os.environ.get('H') or 0))
    d=float(os.environ.get('D') or 0)
    print('true' if (h>0 and w>0 and h>=w and 0<d<=60.5) else 'false')
except Exception:
    print('false')
")
fi

# Build metadata JSON safely using python3 (avoids shell escaping issues)
METADATA=$(IS_SHORT="$IS_SHORT" python3 << 'PYEOF' - "$ARG_TITLE" "$ARG_DESCRIPTION" "${ARG_TAGS:-[]}" "$VISIBILITY" "$CATEGORY_ID"
import sys, json, os, re

title = sys.argv[1]
description = sys.argv[2]
tags_raw = sys.argv[3]
visibility = sys.argv[4]
category_id = sys.argv[5]
is_short = os.environ.get('IS_SHORT', 'false') == 'true'

# Parse tags
try:
    tags = json.loads(tags_raw)
    if not isinstance(tags, list):
        tags = []
except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
    tags = []

# Strip any system-reminder tags that may have leaked in
title = re.sub(r'<system-reminder>.*?</system-reminder>', '', title, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()
description = re.sub(r'<system-reminder>.*?</system-reminder>', '', description, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()

# Add #Shorts ONLY for shorts; long-form must NOT have it (otherwise YouTube may misclassify)
if is_short and "#Shorts" not in description:
    description += "\n\n#Shorts"

metadata = {
    "snippet": {
        "title": title[:100],
        "description": description,
        "tags": [str(t) for t in tags][:30],
        "categoryId": category_id
    },
    "status": {
        "privacyStatus": visibility,
        "selfDeclaredMadeForKids": False
    }
}

print(json.dumps(metadata))
PYEOF
)

if [ -z "$METADATA" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Failed to build video metadata"}'
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 1: Initiate resumable upload (small request, short timeout, retried)
UPLOAD_URL=$(curl -s -D - -o "$WORKDIR/init_body.txt" -X POST \
  --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 60 --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 \
  "https://www.googleapis.com/upload/youtube/v3/videos?uploadType=resumable&part=snippet,status" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Upload-Content-Type: video/mp4" \
  -H "X-Upload-Content-Length: $VIDEO_SIZE" \
  -d "$METADATA" | grep -i 'location:' | sed 's/[Ll]ocation: //;s/\r//')

if [ -z "$UPLOAD_URL" ]; then
  INIT_BODY=$(cat "$WORKDIR/init_body.txt" 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')
  echo "{\"error\": \"Failed to initiate upload\", \"details\": $(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" <<< "$INIT_BODY")}"
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 2: Upload the video — long timeouts for big files; -T streams the file (better than --data-binary for huge files)
UPLOAD_RESP=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X PUT "$UPLOAD_URL" \
  --connect-timeout 60 --max-time 14400 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: video/mp4" \
  -T "$VIDEO_FILE")

HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$UPLOAD_RESP" | tail -1)
RESP_BODY=$(echo "$UPLOAD_RESP" | sed '$d')

rm -rf "$WORKDIR"

if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "201" ]; then
  VIDEO_ID=$(echo "$RESP_BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if [ "$IS_SHORT" = "true" ]; then
    VIDEO_URL_OUT="https://youtube.com/shorts/$VIDEO_ID"
  else
    VIDEO_URL_OUT="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=$VIDEO_ID"
  fi
  echo "{\"video_id\": \"$VIDEO_ID\", \"status\": \"uploaded\", \"visibility\": \"$VISIBILITY\", \"is_short\": $IS_SHORT, \"url\": \"$VIDEO_URL_OUT\"}"
else
  echo "{\"error\": \"Upload failed (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)\", \"is_short\": $IS_SHORT, \"details\": $(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" <<< "$RESP_BODY")}"
fi
